title: Iron Law, Success Criteria, and Gates contradict auto-approve behavior
rationale: Three sections still assert mandatory human approval with no exceptions. Deferred to Phase 3 which is scoped to update these sections.

title: Signal #2 and #3 overlap on high complexity overrides
rationale: highComplexity fires on complexityOverride === high, and complexityOverride fires on !== null. Both fire for high overrides. Signal #2 should check phase.complexity === high only (no OR clause) to make signals distinct.

title: Asymmetric signal value types in JSON schema
rationale: highComplexity stores boolean result, complexityOverride stores actual value. Asymmetry could confuse implementors.

title: Different phase names in auto-approve report examples
rationale: Procedure example says 'Setup Infrastructure', Examples section says 'Core Scanner'. Independent but may confuse.

title: Task threshold 15 hardcoded in 4 places
rationale: If threshold changes, 4 locations need updating. Minor since spec explicitly says hardcoded for now.

title: Asymmetric report formats between auto-approve and pause paths
rationale: Different complexity line formats. Fine for readability, decision record JSON is the machine artifact.
